description_long: This object is a roughly rectangular stone block with a coarse, weathered surface and a prominent spiral or helical groove carved along one of its longer faces. The stone appears to be solid and heavy, with irregular edges and a generally uneven texture, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The spiral groove is the most distinctive feature, giving the block a decorative or functional aspect, possibly as part of a column or architectural element. The other faces are mostly flat but show signs of chipping and natural wear, with no visible engravings or inscriptions. The coloration is a mix of light gray and beige tones, typical of limestone or sandstone. This block would likely be found in an outdoor urban or historical setting, such as a park, plaza, or near an old building.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with a spiral groove carved along one face and a rough, weathered surface in light gray-beige tones.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the spiral groove running vertically along the face, with rough, chipped edges and a coarse stone texture.
description_view_1: This side view shows the block's rectangular profile, with the spiral groove visible along the left edge and a mostly flat, unadorned surface.
description_view_2: The back view reveals a largely flat, featureless stone surface with some natural irregularities and no visible carvings.
description_view_3: This view displays another flat side of the block, showing minor chipping and weathering but no decorative features.
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows significant roughness, chipping, and minor discoloration, indicating prolonged exposure to weather and possible foot traffic.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or possibly part of a decorative row or boundary
